definition: Number of epochs analysed in the recording
Epoch length of the recording
Mean activity over the least-active 5 hours of the average day
Mean activity over the most-active 10 hours of the average day
Relative amplitude of the rest-activity rhythm
Interdaily stability: strength of coupling of the rhythm to the 24 h day
Intradaily variability: fragmentation of the rest-activity rhythm
Autocorrelation-based predictability of the rhythm at the 24 h lag
MESOR: the rhythm-adjusted mean of the fitted 24 h cosine
Amplitude: half the peak-to-trough extent of the fitted cosine
Acrophase: clock time of the cosine peak (circular)
Proportion of variance explained by the single cosine
F statistic of the zero-amplitude (no-rhythm) test
range_interpretation: non-negative; larger = stronger evidence of a rhythm

definition: Denominator degrees of freedom of the rhythmicity F-test
p-value of the zero-amplitude F-test
Percent of variance the single cosine explains (100 * R-squared)
Whether a rhythm is detected at the 0.05 level
Dominant free-running period from the Lomb-Scargle periodogram
Lomb-Scargle spectral power at the dominant period
False-alarm probability of the periodogram peak (Baluev)
Lower bound of the bootstrap period confidence interval
Upper bound of the bootstrap period confidence interval
Bootstrap standard error of the period estimate
Dominant period from the chi-square (Sokolove-Bushell) periodogram
Peak Qp statistic of the chi-square periodogram
Significance of the chi-square periodogram peak (Sidak-corrected)
Overall detrended-fluctuation scaling exponent
Short-time-scale DFA exponent (below the crossover)
Long-time-scale DFA exponent (above the crossover)
Generalized Hurst exponent at q = 2 (equals the DFA exponent)
Width of the multifractal singularity spectrum
Area under the multiscale sample-entropy curve (complexity index)
Slope of sample entropy across coarse-graining scales
range_interpretation: sign indicates rising or falling complexity with scale

definition: Rest-to-active state transition rate
Active-to-rest state transition rate
Rest-to-active state transition probability
Active-to-rest state transition probability
Sleep Regularity Index: how consistently the sleep/wake state repeats 24 h apart (requires sleep_state)
formula_units: 200*P - 100, where P is the fraction of epoch pairs 24 h apart in the same state; index points
range_interpretation: -100 to 100; 100 = perfectly regular, 0 = random, higher = more regular

definition: Social jet lag: mid-sleep difference between free and work days (requires sleep_periods)
Mid-sleep on work days
Mid-sleep on free days
Mean Locomotor Inactivity During Sleep ultradian period (requires sleep_periods)
Mean Munich Rhythmicity Index of the LIDS cycles (requires sleep_periods)
